mission.title: Install and Configure Kmesh on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kmesh is a high-performance service mesh data plane based on eBPF and programmable kernel, designed to enhance traffic management, security, and monitoring without requiring changes to application code. Installing Kmesh allows you to leverage its low overhead and sidecarless architecture for efficient service communication.

steps.title: Create Kmesh Namespace
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for Kmesh to operate in.

steps.title: Apply Kmesh Manifests
steps.description: Next, apply the Kmesh manifests from the GitHub repository. This will deploy the necessary components.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description: After applying the manifests, verify that the Kmesh pods are running successfully.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kmesh will show the Kmesh pods in a running state when you execute `kubectl get pods`. Additionally, the resource limits and monitoring configurations should be applied correctly to ensure optimal performance.

uninstall.title: Remove Kmesh Resources
uninstall.description: To uninstall Kmesh, delete the resources you applied earlier.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kmesh
upgrade.description: To upgrade Kmesh, apply the new version of the manifests. Ensure you specify the correct version.

troubleshooting.title: Pods Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: Check the logs of the Kmesh pods to identify any issues with the startup process.

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that your cluster has enough resources (CPU, Memory) allocated for Kmesh to run.

troubleshooting.title: Network Policies
troubleshooting.description: Verify that there are no network policies blocking traffic to or from Kmesh components.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster version 1.24 or higher and kubectl installed on your machine.
